Ad Text:
At AustralianSuper, we truly care about our colleagues.​ We know work and life are intertwined. That's why we support the diverse needs of everyone and have policies that enable us all to thrive and be truly flexible. We ensure diversity is celebrated for the opportunity it provides us all to learn and grow and deliver better outcomes for members. We are dedicated to building a high-performance environment underpinned by a strong risk culture, ensuring we deliver the best outcomes for our members.

Your New Role

As a Member Portfolio Business Analyst, you'll play a key role in helping AustralianSuper deliver meaningful change for members by supporting portfolio planning, prioritisation and delivery across the Member Domain.

Reporting to the Senior Manager - Portfolio Planning, you'll work closely with sponsors, initiative owners, subject matter experts and enabling teams to assess, shape and progress initiatives through the Member Portfolio. You'll bring strong business analysis capability to support discovery, define requirements, assess impacts and ensure initiatives are set up for success.

This role sits at the intersection of strategy, delivery and continuous improvement, with a strong focus on collaboration, value‑based decision making and simplifying how we operate to deliver better member outcomes.

Key responsibilities include:

Supporting Member Portfolio Planning activities, including backlog management, triage and prioritisation
Managing the domain backlog, engagement hub and front‑door processes
Supporting initiative assessment, discovery and prioritisation using data‑led frameworks
Partnering with initiative owners to develop clear problem definitions, business cases and benefits
Conducting high‑level business analysis, including documenting business, process and information requirements
Supporting portfolio reporting, impact assessments and change governance
Contributing to efficiency, simplification and standardised ways of working across the Member Domain
Building trusted relationships with stakeholders across the Fund to enable successful delivery

What You'll Need

5+ years working in a general Business Analysis
Strong experience in superannuation operations essential
Extensive experience in business analysis, ideally within superannuation or financial services
Strong understanding of portfolio, initiative and change management practices
Demonstrated experience eliciting, analysing and documenting business and process requirements
Proven ability to engage, influence and manage senior and cross‑functional stakeholders
Experience supporting discovery initiatives, business cases and benefits realisation
Strong analytical, problem‑solving and communication skills
Ability to work across multiple initiatives, prioritise effectively and operate with a risk‑aware mindset
Relevant tertiary qualification and/or formal training in Business Analysis

Life at AustralianSuper

AustralianSuper is committed to colleague development, and we support our people with ongoing learning, coaching and training, as well as career opportunities across our expanding global organisation. We offer generous leave entitlements and promote a blended working environment in which all roles can flex, and we're happy to discuss what this looks like for you.

We cultivate a workplace that champions safety, respect, inclusiveness and diversity. We are committed to supporting our diverse workforce in a way that is inclusive and embraces diversity in all its forms. If you require any reasonable adjustments to the recruitment process or the role, please let our recruitment team know.

What's Next

Apply now, if you share our values of Energy, Integrity, Generosity of Spirit and Excellent Outcomes and would like the opportunity to work in a challenging, growing and rapidly evolving team to deliver outstanding results.

AustralianSuper uses AI to review resumes and conduct initial phone interviews. This involves processing your resume, cover letter, and voice recording in Australia and the USA to help assess your suitability for the applied role. When invited to an AI phone screening, you may opt for a traditional phone screening instead. For information on how we handle your personal data, please refer to our Candidate Privacy Policy.

https://www.australiansuper.com/careers/candidate-privacy-notice

Australian or New Zealand citizenship or Australian permanent residency status is required.

Agencies please note: this vacancy is being managed directly by AustralianSuper's Talent Acquisition team. We will contact our preferred agency partners should we require additional support. Thank you.

RPS is seeking a Data and Reporting Analyst to join our growing and vibrant Digital team, supporting data-driven project delivery for a range of government and corporate clients. This is a key opportunity to contribute to impactful digital transformation initiatives through intuitive, interactive and strategic data solutions.

As a Data and Reporting Analyst, you'll help our clients unlock the value of their data by designing, building and optimising business intelligence reports and dashboards that enable smart decision-making. You’ll work across a diverse range of digital projects and corporate applications, delivering new insights and enhancing existing systems.

Key Responsibilities

Adept in developing, publishing and scheduling Power BI reports as per the business requirements.

Proficient with inspecting, cleansing, transforming, and analysing raw data to discover useful information, inform conclusions, and support effective decision-making.

Responsible for designing and building scalable data models and enabling advanced analytic capabilities that provide meaningful business value through easy-to-comprehend data visualizations.

Ability to collaborate with key stakeholders to deliver relevant insights based on identified business requirements.

Ability to work independently through unstructured tasks.

Strong analytical skills, excellent attention to detail, strong interpersonal skills as well as written and verbal communication skills.

Qualifications / Experience

Mandatory

Strong hands-on experience with Microsoft Power BI, including data modelling, DAX, and report development

Familiarity with modern Microsoft data platforms, such as Microsoft Fabric and Azure Synapse / Data Lake

Minimum 2 years’ experience in a data-specific role (e.g. business intelligence, analytics, reporting, or data engineering)

Tertiary qualification in a relevant field (e.g. Data Analytics, Computer Science, Information Systems) or industry-recognised certification (e.g. Microsoft Certified: Data Analyst Associate)

Highly Desirable:

Exposure to data integration tools (e.g. Azure Data Factory, Power Platform)

Experience working within government, utilities, or regulated environments

Understanding of data governance, security models, and self-service BI best practices

Proficiency in SQL or scripting languages used for data prep and automation

Strong communication skills and stakeholder engagement experience, especially in cross-functional teams

NV1 Security Clearance.

About us

RPS is a market-leading consultancy delivering innovative digital solutions across Australia and beyond. Our Digital team is at the forefront of data transformation, partnering with government and enterprise clients to turn complexity into clarity through meaningful data and actionable insights.

We offer more than just a job, we offer a place to grow, be heard, and make a tangible impact.

What You Can Expect:

Flexible and remote work options to support work–life balance and individual needs

Competitive salary package aligned to experience and capability

A genuinely collaborative team culture where curiosity, initiative and innovation are encouraged

Ongoing professional development, with access to industry leaders, certifications and cross-disciplinary learning

Opportunities to work on diverse, high-impact projects across digital, infrastructure, sustainability and the built environment

Involvement in a team actively investing in Microsoft Fabric, AI and modern data platforms as part of its digital evolution

Apply now!

About the Role

We are seeking an experienced Functional Consultant - Dynamics 365 Finance & Operations (F&O) to support and enhance a complex enterprise ERP environment.

This is a senior hands-on functional role focused on improving financial and operational processes through well-designed ERP solutions. You will not be coding, but instead working deeply with the platform to configure, optimise, and evolve Dynamics 365 F&O in line with business, finance, and compliance requirements.

You will act as a key bridge between Finance, Operations, Product, and Technology teams, ensuring the ERP platform supports accurate financial processing, strong internal controls, and scalable operational workflows.

A background in accounting, finance, or business operations is highly valued, and experience in healthcare or regulated environments is a strong advantage.

What You'll Be Doing

You will own the functional lifecycle of ERP changes across Dynamics 365 Finance & Operations, including discovery through to deployment and continuous improvement.

Key responsibilities include:

Leading workshops with Finance and Operations stakeholders to understand requirements, controls, and reporting needs
Mapping current and future state processes with a strong focus on governance, compliance, and efficiency
Translating business and regulatory needs into functional designs, user stories, and acceptance criteria
Making practical decisions on configuration vs customisation, balancing system capability, risk, and sustainability
Configuring core Dynamics 365 F&O capabilities such as financial structures, workflows, approvals, posting logic, master data, and security
Ensuring strong segregation of duties, access control, and audit readiness across the ERP environment
Supporting integration design with banking, procurement, reporting, and other enterprise systems
Contributing to data migration activities including mapping, validation, reconciliation, and sign-off
Supporting testing, UAT, defect resolution, and release readiness
Playing a key role in cutover planning, deployment activities, and hypercare support
Driving continuous improvement through analysis of system usage, issues, and business feedback
Supporting training, documentation, and adoption activities across business users

About You

You are a strong ERP Functional Consultant who understands both business processes and system configuration, and you're comfortable operating in complex enterprise environments.

You will bring:

Solid experience delivering Dynamics 365 Finance & Operations (or Dynamics AX) solutions
Strong understanding of ERP financial and operational processes
Hands-on experience configuring modules such as General Ledger, AP/AR, Procurement, Inventory, Projects, or Supply Chain
Strong grasp of financial controls, approvals, and governance structures
Experience with workflows, posting logic, security roles, and ERP configurations
Ability to translate business and accounting requirements into system design
Strong testing, UAT, and defect management experience
Excellent stakeholder engagement skills across Finance, Operations, and Technology

Nice to Have

Background in accounting, finance, or business operations (highly regarded)
Experience in healthcare, government, or other regulated environments
Exposure to ERP integrations and data tools (DMF, OData, etc.)
Experience supporting period-end or year-end financial cycles
Familiarity with agile or product-based delivery models
Microsoft certifications (MB-310, MB-330, MB-300)

Why This Role Matters

This role is central to ensuring the organisation's finance and operations backbone is accurate, compliant, and efficient. You'll be helping shape how critical ERP processes run - from financial reporting and procurement through to operational workflows - ensuring the system is stable, controlled, and scalable for the future.

To apply, please reference job number #273688

To be considered for the role click the 'apply' button or for more information about this and other opportunities please contact Aditi Yadav on 02 9464 5530 or email: ayadav@paxus.com.au and quote the above job reference number.

Paxus values diversity and welcomes applications from Indigenous Australians, people from diverse cultural and linguistic backgrounds and people living with a disability. If you require an adjustment to the recruitment process, including the application form in an alternate format, please contact me on the above contact details.

Company: Talent – Specialists in tech, transformation & beyond
Ad Text:
We are seeking a highly experienced and strategic professional to lead the development and delivery of key initiatives within a complex insurance policy strategy program. This role offers the opportunity to work at a senior level, providing trusted advice to executives and driving outcomes across a diverse stakeholder landscape.

It is a 6 months contract role.

Key Responsibilities
Lead the development and delivery of strategic products, including executive briefings and advisory materials
Provide thought leadership through environmental scanning, research, and strategic analysis
Oversee planning, implementation, and reporting activities across the program
Lead coordination across multiple business areas to support delivery of complex initiatives
Support leadership in driving continuous improvement and organisational effectiveness
Key Skills & Experience
Demonstrated experience in delivering and implementing large-scale and complex policy initiatives
Strong background in governance, financial monitoring, reporting, and project coordination
Exceptional strategic planning skills, with the ability to translate strategy into actionable plans
Sound understanding of organisational, political, and regulatory environments
Experience working in the public sector

Apply now to secure an interview or contact Anita Fonseka at anita.fonseka@talentinternational.com

WHO WE ARE

Known best as the 'Undisputed King of Trainers', JD Sports reigns supreme as one of the biggest and best global Sports Fashion brands across footwear and apparel.

Founded in 1981 in Greater Manchester UK, JD Group has achieved remarkable growth through rapid global expansion, now standing as the leading global sports fashion retailer. With over 3,400 stores worldwide, we strive to inspire the emerging generation through a captivating connection to the universal culture of Sport, Music, and Fashion.

In 2017, JD Sports first launched within the Australian market and has since expanded to 60+ stores across all Australian states and New Zealand, with plans for continued future growth. Our mission is to offer unparalleled service and style to all our customers.

Working at JD Sports is a state of mind, an attitude and way of challenging the norm. We do not conform; we create our own path and are elite within our field. And now, we’re seeking talented and passionate individuals to join our team.

THE ROLE

We are looking for a commercially driven and strategically sharp Head of Performance Marketing to lead our acquisition strategy across APAC. This is a leadership role responsible for driving revenue growth, efficiency and market share across both paid and organic channels.

Leading a high-performing team across Paid Media, Affiliates, Paid Social and SEO, you will own significant budget investment and be accountable for delivering strong commercial outcomes across key platforms including Google, Meta, TikTok, Snapchat and emerging AI-driven discovery channels.

This role requires a true player-coach, suiting someone equally comfortable setting strategy and getting hands-on within platforms, data and performance optimisation. The role will be responsible for:

Strategy & Commercial Performance

Own and deliver the end-to-end performance marketing strategy across APAC, aligned to business growth objectives

Drive profitable revenue growth, embedding margin and contribution into all channel decisions

Lead an incrementality-first approach, ensuring investment decisions are grounded in true performance vs attributed metrics

Integrate channel strategy with trading calendars, promotions and wider digital activity

Channel & Platform Leadership

Oversee performance across Google (Search, Shopping, PMax, YouTube), Meta, TikTok, Snapchat and programmatic

Continuously evolve the channel mix and audience strategies to maximise growth

Lead the organic search strategy, driving sustainable traffic and revenue through SEO and content optimisation

Develop and execute a Generative Engine Optimisation (GEO) strategy to ensure visibility in AI-driven search environments

Measurement & Optimisation

Own the measurement and attribution framework, including MMM, MTA and first-party data strategies

Ensure spend is optimised against ROAS, COS and revenue targets across all markets

Leadership & Stakeholder Management

Lead, coach and develop a high-performing, data-driven team and build strong partnerships with platforms (Google, Meta, TikTok, Snapchat) and key external partners

Work cross-functionally with Trading, CRM, Data, Finance and Brand teams to deliver integrated outcomes

WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR?

Proven experience leading performance marketing in-house within Retail or eCommerce

Strong commercial acumen, with ownership of significant media budgets and revenue targets

Deep expertise in paid media, SEO and advanced measurement (MMM, MTA, attribution)

Hands-on experience across key platforms, not just agency management

Ability to translate complex data into clear, actionable commercial insights

A player-coach mindset with a passion for building and developing high-performing teams

Experience operating across APAC markets with an understanding of regional nuance

WHAT’S IN IT FOR YOU?

Birthday leave

Competitive Salary

One month paid parental leave

Referral bonus scheme

Training and development to evolve and shape your career

Opportunity to progress across all areas of the business including Retail, Support Office and our Distribution Centre

Treat yourself with our attractive staff discount! This can also be used for friends and family

Access to our Employee Assistance Program & Mental Health champions

We are passionate about connecting with driven and skilled individuals who are invested in their development, so that together we can evolve our brand's success, alongside evolving your career.

JD values diversity and inclusion and champions a culture where everyone feels, valued, included, and celebrated.

What are you waiting for? Apply today and kick start your JD Sports Journey.

Please note, by applying for this role, you consent to us completing a Work Rights check to verify your working rights where required.

We are an inclusive employer committed to fostering a diverse and accessible workplace. We encourage applications from Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples, people with disabilities, LGBTQIA+ individuals, people of all ages, and those from culturally and linguistically diverse backgrounds.

Your work days are brighter here.

We’re obsessed with making hard work pay off, for our people, our customers, and the world around us. As a Fortune 500 company and a leading AI platform for managing people, money, and agents, we’re shaping the future of work so teams can reach their potential and focus on what matters most. The minute you join, you’ll feel it. Not just in the products we build, but in how we show up for each other. Our culture is rooted in integrity, empathy, and shared enthusiasm. We’re in this together, tackling big challenges with bold ideas and genuine care. We look for curious minds and courageous collaborators who bring sun-drenched optimism and drive. Whether you're building smarter solutions, supporting customers, or creating a space where everyone belongs, you’ll do meaningful work with Workmates who’ve got your back. In return, we’ll give you the trust to take risks, the tools to grow, the skills to develop and the support of a company invested in you for the long haul. So, if you want to inspire a brighter work day for everyone, including yourself, you’ve found a match in Workday, and we hope to be a match for you too.

About the Team

The Workday Adaptive Planning product team designs high-performance Enterprise Performance Management (EPM) solutions that replace manual spreadsheets with AI-driven, active planning. By leveraging their patented Elastic Hypercube Technology, they empower finance and operations teams to perform complex, multi-dimensional modeling with speed and agility. Their work centers on democratizing data across organizations, ensuring that every department can collaborate on a single, actionable source of truth.

About the Role

Workday is reimagining the future of financial planning — and this role sits at the heart of that transformation. As a Product Manager for Next-Gen User Experience on Adaptive Planning, you will own the product vision, strategy, and roadmap for the next generation of our planning platform's user experience, shaping how thousands of finance professionals engage with Workday's industry-leading tools.

You will bridge the gap between deep customer empathy and technical excellence — partnering with engineering, design, and business stakeholders to translate complex customer needs into elegant, intuitive product experiences. This is a high-impact role for a PM who is equally comfortable presenting a strategic vision to executives and diving deep into user research to understand what truly matters to our customers.

How This Role Contributes to Our Broader Vision
Adaptive Planning is central to Workday's strategy to deliver the most intelligent, connected, and user-centric financial planning platform in the market. The next generation of our UX will be a foundational shift in how customers plan, analyze, and act on financial data.
In this role, you will:
•    Champion a user-first philosophy across the product organization, ensuring that validated customer insight drives every major product decision.
•    Serve as the connective tissue between business outcomes, technical architecture, and end-user experience — ensuring that what we build is both technically excellent and genuinely delightful to use.
•    Establish Adaptive Planning as the benchmark for enterprise UX in financial planning software.
 

Product Vision & Strategy

Define and own the product vision, strategy, and roadmap for the next-gen UX of Adaptive Planning, grounded in customer insight
Drive solution design for each release based on user requirements, technical architecture, and measurable business outcomes.
Guide product teams toward high-value OKRs, ensuring the team is solving the right problems and delivering meaningful results.
Cross-Functional Collaboration & Delivery

Collaborate with Workday's business product owners, customers, partners, development teams including architecture, consultants, operations, and product teams to understand their desired business outcomes and priorities.
Work closely with product development teams through design, development, testing, release, and maintenance to ensure quality and on-time delivery.
Coordinate and align solution design across multiple scrum teams operating in a sophisticated distributed software environment.
Leverage AI-powered tools and workflows to enhance collaboration with development teams — accelerating specification writing, technical documentation, and requirements synthesis, and enabling faster, higher-quality iteration between product and engineering.
Stakeholder & Community Engagement

Lead technical and strategic discussions with key internal and external stakeholders and customers, representing the product roadmap with clarity and conviction.
Communicate and evangelize the product area among the Workday community and at Workday events, building enthusiasm and alignment around the next-gen UX vision.
Manage stakeholder expectations proactively, ensuring leadership, engineering, and customer-facing teams are informed and aligned throughout the product lifecycle.

About You

Basic Qualifications

5+ years of experience in product management, defining and delivering SaaS and/or cloud-based enterprise software products or features.
Bachelor's degree in a relevant discipline such as Business, Computer Science, or a related field — or equivalent practical experience.
Other qualifications

Business Influence: Proven ability to build consensus and drive alignment across product teams and senior stakeholders, using data, customer narratives, and strategic frameworks to make a compelling case for your roadmap.
Cross-Team Collaboration: Track record of leading effectively across Engineering, Design, and Go-to-Market teams to define and deliver complex product features in a distributed environment.
Customer Advocacy: Deep commitment to representing the voice of the customer in every product decision — through continuous user research, feedback analysis, and direct customer engagement.
Customer Empathy: A natural inclination to seek out and deeply understand customer pain points, translating them into product bets that meaningfully improve the user experience.
Data Analysis: Solid skills in using product analytics, usage data, and qualitative research to derive actionable insights that inform prioritization, strategy, and success measurement.
Decision Making: Comfort navigating ambiguity and making sound, well-reasoned decisions that balance user needs, business goals, and technical feasibility — and communicating those decisions transparently.
Domain Knowledge: Understanding of enterprise financial planning, FP&A workflows, or adjacent SaaS markets — with an appetite to go deep on the trends and competitive landscape shaping the space.
Research: Experience using both qualitative and quantitative research methodologies to identify product opportunities, validate hypotheses, and understand user behavior.
Stakeholder Management: Demonstrated ability to manage expectations across a broad range of stakeholder groups — from engineering leads to C-suite sponsors — with clarity and professionalism.
Strategic Thinking: Ability to zoom out from day-to-day delivery and contribute meaningfully to the long-term vision of the product, identifying emerging opportunities and potential inflection points.

Our Approach to Flexible Work
 

With Flex Work, we’re combining the best of both worlds: in-person time and remote. Our approach enables our teams to deepen connections, maintain a strong community, and do their best work. We know that flexibility can take shape in many ways, so rather than a number of required days in-office each week, we simply spend at least half (50%) of our time each quarter in the office or in the field with our customers, prospects, and partners (depending on role). This means you'll have the freedom to create a flexible schedule that caters to your business, team, and personal needs, while being intentional to make the most of time spent together. Those in our remote "home office" roles also have the opportunity to come together in our offices for important moments that matter.

At Workday, we are committed to providing an accessible and inclusive hiring experience where all candidates can fully demonstrate their skills. If you require assistance or an accommodation at any point, please email accommodations@workday.com.

Are you being referred to one of our roles? If so, ask your connection at Workday about our Employee Referral process!

At Workday, we value our candidates’ privacy and data security.  Workday will never ask candidates to apply to jobs through websites that are not Workday Careers. 

Please be aware of sites that may ask for you to input your data in connection with a job posting that appears to be from Workday but is not.

In addition, Workday will never ask candidates to pay a recruiting fee, or pay for consulting or coaching services, in order to apply for a job at Workday.

Job Title: Head of Research, Innovation and Predictive Capability
Ad Text:
CFA is an emergency service that places the community at the centre of everything we do. We work to protect lives and property across Victoria, every hour of the day.

 

Our organisation is made up of employees working alongside more than 51,000 volunteers. We value the different skills, experience and viewpoints that people bring, because they strengthen how we operate and support the services Victorians rely on.

We aim to be a workplace where people feel supported to do meaningful work, develop professionally and contribute to outcomes that matter to the people and places we serve.

To learn more about who we are and the work we do, visit our About Us page.

About the Role

The Head of Research, Innovation and Predictive Capability is a newly created executive leadership role reporting to the Deputy Chief Officer, Fire Risk Research and Community Preparedness. This position provides strategic leadership across CFA, government and the broader sector to strengthen bushfire management practices in Victoria and reduce the impacts of bushfires on people, the environment and the economy.

The role leads key functions including the CFA research program, sector innovation initiatives, the joint-agency Predictive Services Program and bushfire risk analysis. This includes guiding research priorities, driving innovation and the adoption of new technologies, strengthening predictive capability and supporting evidence-based decision making through data, field risk assessments and risk modelling.

As a member of the senior leadership team, you will influence research and innovation across the sector, build strong partnerships with government, agencies and research organisations and provide strategic advice to support CFA's bushfire risk management responsibilities. The role also contributes to the broader strategic direction of the organisation while fostering collaboration and leading with professionalism, integrity and accountability.

About you

You are a highly experienced and strategic manager with strong expertise in natural resource management, fire-related research or a related field. A tertiary qualification in a relevant discipline is essential, with a PhD or management qualification highly regarded.

You bring extensive experience leading the development and delivery of complex programs and initiatives, with a proven ability to translate strategy into outcomes that support organisational goals. Established networks in research, innovation and predictive services are essential, with connections in the fire and emergency services sector highly desirable.

You are a skilled leader, capable of building and guiding high-performing teams and have a strong track record in developing effective partnerships, networks and community-centred initiatives. Your exceptional communication and interpersonal skills enable you to navigate complex matters and influence internal and external stakeholders with credibility and impact.

Why choose CFA

Meaningful Purpose: Your contribution truly makes a difference
Flexibility: Hybrid work options with flexible work arrangements
Discounts: Emergency Memberlink discounts on various services
Wellbeing Focus: Health Ready programs, flu vaccinations
Member Assistance Program: Access support across 8 service pathways 

Your Application

Click the ‘Apply' button to access our online application portal. You will be prompted to provide your personal and contact details and to respond to employment-related questions. When applying for a position with CFA, please include a cover letter and resume that clearly demonstrate your qualifications, experience and the key attributes and skills relevant to the role.

Pre-employment Checks

Offers of employment will only be made to candidates who are able to successfully complete a Nationally Coordinated Criminal History Check, Working Rights Check and hold a valid Working with Children Check.  You will also be required to provide at least two professional references.

Applications close: 11:59pm 3 May 2026

CFA is committed to creating and maintaining a diverse, inclusive, and safe volunteer and work environment. Our aim is to have a volunteer and paid workforce that reflects the community it serves.  First Nations people, women, people of all ages, with disabilities and culturally and linguistically diverse people are encouraged to apply. 

If at any stage of the recruitment and selection process you require an alternative format to the application material or reasonable adjustment to the interview arrangements, please don't hesitate to contact the person listed on the ad or alternatively, email recruitment@cfa.vic.gov.au

The Country Fire Authority (CFA) values your privacy. We will use the information you provide when you are applying for employment with us for the purpose of recruitment, selection, evaluation and appointment and to report on anonymised key metrics (e.g. diversity). The information we collect will be handled in accordance with privacy laws, including the Privacy and Data Protection Act 2014. You can view our Privacy Statement here. For access and correction of the information we have collected, contact recruitment@cfa.vic.gov.au

Apply now

Are you ready to lead a major systems transformation and help shape the future of Aged Care?

For Purpose Aged Care are seeking a Systems Transformation Project Manager to lead an organisation‑wide workforce and finance systems transformation that frees our frontline and support teams to focus on what matters most — delivering exceptional resident care.

This role is pivotal in modernising how we work, enabling safe, scalable growth, and embedding user‑friendly systems that create more time to care.

 
Why This Roles Matters
 

You will lead one of the most significant transformation programs in our organisation’s history. Your work will streamline processes, uplift data and system capability, and deliver integrated HR, WFM, payroll and finance systems that improve visibility, accuracy and operational efficiency.

By championing co‑design, strong governance and practical adoption, you’ll help our teams embrace new ways of working that directly enhance the resident experience.

 
 
About the Role

Lead end‑to‑end delivery of the workforce and finance systems transformation program

Own the integrated delivery plan across HR, onboarding, WFM, payroll and finance

Establish and run program governance, ensuring fast, high‑quality decision‑making

Manage scope, budget, risks, dependencies and change control

Coordinate cross‑functional workstreams across process, data, technology, testing and change

Oversee data readiness, migration, integrations and end‑to‑end testing

Lead cutover planning, rehearsals, hypercare and transition to BAU

Partner with operations and Homes to co‑design practical, frontline‑friendly solutions

Drive adoption through training, communications, pilots and wave rollouts

Track benefits such as time saved, payroll accuracy and workforce visibility

Embed sustainable governance, documentation and continuous improvement

Support safe work practices and contribute to a positive, values‑driven culture

 
What you will bring

Tertiary qualification in Business, Technology, HR, Finance or related field

Project management qualification desirable

Hands‑on leadership of complex HRIS, WFM, rostering or payroll transformations

Strong delivery discipline across planning, RAID management and execution cadence

Expertise in data migration, integrations, testing and cutover

Ability to build alignment across executives, operations, IT, HR, Finance and vendors

Clear, confident communication across technical, operational and frontline audiences

Experience in aged care, healthcare or multi‑site operational environments (desirable)

Ability to travel to Homes and interstate as required

Current Police Check and compliance

 
Why Join For Purpose
 

At For Purpose Aged Care, you’ll be part of a mission‑driven organisation that values innovation, collaboration and care — for both our residents and our people. We invest in modern systems and capability to make work smarter, not harder.

 
 
Employee Benefits

Salary Packaging – save up to $15,900 per year (Go Salary)

Access to state‑of‑the‑art technology and tools

Regular team lunches and events

Discounts with JB Hi‑Fi, Endota Spa and more

CommBank workplace banking & novated leasing options

Salary sacrifice into super + HESTA benefits

Free, confidential Employee Assistance Program (EAP)

Access to Ausmed Passport for professional development

 
 
Ready to Make an Impact
 

If you’re passionate about leading meaningful transformation and enabling teams to deliver exceptional care, we’d love to hear from you!

For further information about For Purpose Aged Care head to: https://fpagedcare.org.au/

About the Opportunity

As the Service Product Lead for Workday in ETS, you will be responsible for overseeing the operational management, integration, and technical optimisation of the Workday HRIS platform. This role focuses on ensuring that Workday is seamlessly integrated with other enterprise systems, delivers reliable platform performance, and meets the technical requirements of People Experience and business operations. The SPL will collaborate closely with HRIS and ETS teams to manage platform services, monitor system health, drive integrations and provide technical support to ensure a stable and efficient ecosystem.

You will actively scan the external landscape to identify emerging technologies and assess their relevance and potential value to the business needs.

The Service Product Lead ensures the seamless integration of all IT service components and systems, aligning with Flight Centre Travel Group’s strategic goals. You will translate high-level business objectives into actionable roadmaps, balancing long-term aspirations with short-term deliverables.

You will lead through influence across cross-functional teams to deliver high-quality, user-centric services.

 

What you will be doing:

Platform Management & Integration

Own the technical integration of Workday with other enterprise systems, ensuring seamless data flow and system interoperability.

Service Delivery & Optimisation

Oversee platform updates, releases, and enhancements, ensuring minimal disruption and aligning technical changes with business needs.

Product and Service Roadmap

Define and manage the IT product roadmap in line with business objectives and user feedback
Monitor the external environment to gather intelligence on emerging technologies

Service Ownership

Deal with problems/issues, conduct root cause analysis, manage resolutions with corrective actions and disseminate lessons learned, driving improvement
Monitor the health and performance of the Workday platform

Stakeholder Engagement

Oversee problems and issues, manages resolution, corrective actios, and the collection and dissemination of information

Governance and Compliance

Ensure all products adhere to regulatory, security and compliance requirements and maintain documentation and service catalogue entries.

Budget and Financial Management

Manage budgeting and cost optimisation

 

What we are looking for:

5+ years of experience in product management
Strong technical knowledge of the Workday platform, including integration capabilities - please outline in your application
Specific knowledge of the HCM product domain
Extensive experience within a Product /Platform Delivery environment
Strong understanding of project, portfolio, Organisational Change Management (OCM), and Agile / ITIL techniques
Familiarity with the functioning of a PMO and a Steering Committee
Travel and Tourism industry knowledge preferred
Product Management Certification
ITIL Certification
A bachelor’s degree in IT, Business or related fields
Industry qualifications in the specified product domain

This role sits at the centre of how the business actually runs.
Own HR systems, data and reporting end-to-end, with a proposed merger adding complexity across integration, data mapping and change.

You’ll sit at the intersection of HR, data and technology, taking ownership of the HRIS (SuccessFactors) and the full people reporting landscape. It’s hands-on and influential.

A proposed merger will bring a layer of complexity across system integration, data mapping and change, suiting someone comfortable working through moving parts and bringing structure where things aren’t fully defined.

The Opportunity.
You’ll own the core HR systems and reporting function end-to-end. It’s a mix of operational depth and project work, with a strong commercial lens.

That means:

Keeping SuccessFactors running cleanly, accurately and efficiently
Lifting data integrity and simplifying how the system is used
Building and evolving dashboards that senior leaders and the Board actually rely on
Translating people data into insights that inform workforce decisions
Leading or supporting system changes, upgrades and integrations
Playing a key role in merger-related system and reporting work
Working across HR, Finance, IT and external vendors to get things done
You.
You don’t need to tick every box, but you’ll recognise yourself in most of this:

Solid experience in HR systems, ideally SuccessFactors or similar
Strong reporting capability, with the ability to turn data into something meaningful
Comfortable owning data accuracy and standing behind it
Experience working through system changes, upgrades or integrations
Able to manage multiple stakeholders without overcomplicating things
Calm in complexity and able to bring structure when things are moving
You’re probably someone who enjoys making things cleaner, simpler and more reliable. You care about getting it right, but you’re also pragmatic enough to keep things moving.

Why this role?
It’s a good role for someone who wants to step beyond pure system administration. You’ll have visibility across the business, exposure to senior stakeholders and a genuine opportunity to shape how people data is used. The merger work adds variety and complexity without turning the role into a pure project seat.

Join our team of business professionals doing meaningful work in an organisation that’s ever evolving. Work with people who care and expand your career with opportunities only a diverse business can provide. With license to see things through your way, and the option to split your time between office and home, RACV is where you’ll make a difference.




About the Opportunity

We’re seeking an experienced Senior Business Analyst to play a key role in delivering membership subscription initiatives with a strong focus on home-related products and services. This role supports strategic growth and optimisation of RACV’s membership offerings, ensuring solutions are customer‑centric, scalable, and deliver tangible value for members.

Partnering closely with senior business and technology stakeholders, you will apply strong analytical, communication, and problem‑solving skills to shape and deliver initiatives across the end‑to‑end subscription lifecycle. Your work will contribute to evolving how members engage with RACV’s home services while supporting broader enterprise transformation objectives.

Come along for a new journey

As a senior member of our Program Management team, you’ll help drive high-quality delivery across both strategic and operational initiatives. Here’s what you’ll be doing:




Lead end-to-end business analysis activities including scoping, planning, requirements gathering and process documentation across waterfall, hybrid and agile delivery environments.

Work extensively on Membership and subscription-related initiatives, supporting regulatory, operational, digital and product transformation projects.

Support data analysis and marketing journeys by considering and commercial modelling insights  and the impact on serviceability, eligibility and personalisation of communication.

Conduct detailed stakeholder and impact analysis across processes, people, technology and ways of working.

Map and design business processes, delivering clear current-state (as-is) and future-state (to-be) models.

Facilitate workshops to elicit business and Technology (including Digital) requirements and translate them into refined, actionable outputs.

Define business metrics, acceptance criteria and success measures to guide solution delivery.

Produce high-quality documentation including business requirements, epics, user stories, integration specifications and dependency mapping.

Develop user-facing artefacts such as work instructions, guides and training materials.

Support transition to BAU, preparing handover documentation and ensuring smooth operational readiness.

 

Who we’re looking for and what you’ll bring

You’ll thrive in a collaborative, people-centred organisation where members are at the heart of everything we do. You’ll bring:




5+ years’ experience as a Business Analyst (or similar) working on complex, end-to-end technology and business transformation initiatives.

Insurance industry and/or subscription experience (required) — with strong knowledge of Membership processes, systems, products or regulatory environments.

Demonstrated experience delivering analysis for Membership projects,

From discovery through optimisation—designing customer‑centric, scalable subscription experiences, navigating complex stakeholder and system landscapes, and delivering measurable growth, retention, and engagement outcomes through continuous evolution rather than one‑off delivery.

Strong capability working in both agile and waterfall methodologies.

Salesforce and relevant job allocation system experience

Experience designing and shaping intuitive, accessible digital experiences by translating user needs and business objectives into clear, actionable requirements

Tertiary qualifications in Computer Science, MIS or a related field (essential).

Experience in member-based or customer-centric environments (advantageous).

Exceptional communication, facilitation and stakeholder management skills.

 

There’s so much on offer when you join RACV with benefits including

Our flexible and informal work environment which helps bring balance between work and home.

Generous discounts on home and car insurance policies and RACV roadside care

Great discounts across all RACV resorts

Discounts from corporate partners that are updated monthly.

 

Our difference

At RACV, we believe that maintaining a diverse and inclusive workplace is an important element in our continued success. We want our people to feel welcome, supported and appreciated for who they are and what they bring to our business. If you require any reasonable adjustments to the recruitment process and/or the role, please let our Talent Acquisition Team know.




Application Process

Applicants will be required to provide evidence of their eligibility to work in Australia, and consent to a criminal record check.

Who We Are

 

iSelect and Compare the Market are trusted, well-known brands dedicated to helping millions of Australians compare and buy personal finance and household products such as insurance, energy, and loans. Together, we provide a seamless, customer-focused experience that makes it easier for Australians to find the right products to suit their needs.

 

 

What You’ll Be Doing 

 

As an Engineering Delivery Lead, you will drive the successful delivery of technical initiatives and continuous improvement across software engineering squads. You will act as the operational link between business and technical teams, ensuring effective execution of project pipelines, release management, and delivery governance.

 

Supporting multiple delivery pods, you will translate strategic intent into clear, actionable work, remove blockers, facilitate agile rituals, and improve flow efficiency to enable high-quality delivery aligned to commercial and customer outcomes.

 

Delivery Execution & Coordination: Lead end-to-end software delivery for cross-functional teams, ensuring timely, high-quality outcomes across the SDLC. Facilitate agile ceremonies, promote delivery best practices, manage backlogs, and translate high-level initiatives into sprint-ready work with clear outcomes.
Stakeholder Management & Collaboration: Partner with Engineering Managers, Product Owners, and Project Managers to set priorities and manage delivery plans. Provide clear stakeholder communication through structured updates and reporting (e.g. RAID), ensuring effective collaboration and handovers between business and technical teams.
Governance & Compliance: Ensure delivery aligns with established governance and CTM Flow, meeting all commercial, security, and regulatory requirements through structured, auditable processes.
Continuous Improvement & Metrics: Monitor delivery health through velocity, risk, and dependency tracking. Proactively address blockers and lead improvements in planning accuracy, estimation, quality, and delivery efficiency.
Team Enablement & Culture: Support Engineering Managers in building team capability and a high-performance culture, fostering continuous learning, agile ways of working, and knowledge sharing across teams.

 

                                                                                                                                          

What’s In It For You

 

At Compare the Market and iSelect, we’re all about helping people make smart choices – and joining us might just be yours.  We offer an environment where your ideas are heard, your contributions are valued, and your growth genuinely matters. Collaboration is part of our DNA, and you’ll be joining a supportive, forward-thinking team where you can do meaningful work (and be recognised for it!) 

 

We’re guided by three shared values that shape how we work, how we treat each other, and how we show up for our customers - Drive, Noble Intent and All Together.

 

Plus, we offer a range of great perks to help you thrive both in and out of work:

 

Vibrant and social community who have annual celebrations, family fun days and regular events
Enjoy flexible work arrangements, including the option of one day working from home each week or a 9-day fortnight
Enjoy additional leave days - ‘ME’ leave and ‘Volunteer Day’ leave
Option to purchase additional leave
Return To Work bonus for returning parents
Quarterly Award and Recognition program

 

 

What Sets You Apart

 

Technology related Degree or relevant industry experience. Formal certification is desirable.

Experience:

3+ years in software delivery roles (e.g., Delivery Lead, Scrum Master or Iteration Manager)
Hands-on experience facilitating agile delivery, managing release cycles, and simultaneously supporting multiple cross-functional technical teams
Understanding of SDLC processes and Agile frameworks (Scrum, Kanban)
Familiarity with modern delivery toolchains (e.g., Jira, Confluence, Azure DevOps)

Skills:

Strong organisational and facilitation skills
Excellent verbal and written communication skills
Natural curiosity and strive for continuous learning and improvement
Ability to efficiently operate within a high-paced environment
High attention to detail and strong documentation skills
Strong problem-solving and collaboration abilities
Ability to lead teams through coaching, influence and embedding agile mindset

My client is a leading Financial Services organisation recruiting for a Dealing Room Risk Senior Manager. They can also consider candidates at a Manager level.
 
Responsibilities:-
 
Partner closely with trading desks to provide independent oversight of market risk across commodities portfolios spanning energy, metals, agriculture and environmental products.
Monitor and interpret risk exposures using a range of metrics (e.g. VaR, stress scenarios, sensitivities), ensuring positions remain within agreed appetite.
Design and enhance risk frameworks, including limits, triggers and early warning indicators to support proactive risk management.
Provide constructive challenge on trading strategies, new products and complex transactions.
Oversee valuation inputs and methodologies, ensuring consistency and integrity across pricing and risk systems.
Deliver clear, insight-led reporting to senior stakeholders, translating market movements into actionable risk views.
Support governance forums and contribute to the ongoing evolution of risk policies and standards.
Work cross-functionally with teams across treasury, finance and technology to embed best practice risk controls.
 
Profile:-
 
6–10 years’ experience in commodities market risk, trading, or a related control function within banking, financial services, or trading environments.
Strong understanding of both physical and financial commodities, including key risk drivers such as logistics, storage and basis risk.
Hands-on experience with market risk methodologies including VaR, stress testing and scenario analysis.
Proven ability to operate as an independent second line, providing credible challenge while building effective stakeholder relationships.
Experience working with risk systems and data tools (e.g. Python, SQL or similar).
High level of commercial acumen, balancing robust risk management with business objectives .
Confident communicator, comfortable engaging with senior stakeholders and influencing decision-making.
Relevant tertiary qualifications in a quantitative discipline; postgraduate study or certifications (e.g. CFA, FRM) viewed favourably.
Familiarity with regulatory frameworks within financial markets or banking environments.
 
PLEASE only apply if you have the above experience. 
 
You must have PR or Citizenship for Australia.

Job Title: Senior Talent Growth and Leadership Partner
Ad Text:
What's the role?

In this position, you will partner closely with HR and business stakeholders to design and deliver high impact, scalable learning interventions that directly support our strategic priorities. Your responsibilities include:

Strategic Capability Planning: Designing and implementing a cohesive annual learning strategy that clarifies desired skills and proficiency levels across all employee cohorts.

Curriculum Design & Delivery(facilitation): Leading the development of innovative learning programs that translate theory into practical, on-the-job skills.

Leadership Development: Engaging with leaders to deliver specific development programs for emerging leaders and specialised workshops.

Supplier & Content Management: Selecting and managing external learning suppliers and maintaining a centralised repository learning resources.

Project Oversight: Planning and prioritising multiple development activities to meet objectives within specified timescales and budgets.

Who are you?

We are looking for a strategic thinker who can translate complex business objectives into effective learning solutions. You should possess:

Consultative Business Partnering: A proactive approach with the ability to challenge the status quo and drive constructive change.

Stakeholder Influence: Exceptional relationship-building skills and the ability to consult effectively with varied levels of leadership.

Design Expertise: Strong knowledge of adult learning principles, blended learning methodologies, and contemporary instructional design models.

Strategic Mindset: The ability to see ahead to future possibilities and prioritise initiatives for the greatest organisational impact.

Effective Communication: Excellent verbal and written skills, with the ability to simplify complex technical concepts for any audience.

Commitment to Excellence: A results-driven attitude that consistently achieves goals and holds self and others accountable to high standards.

Innovative Spirit: An openness to new ideas and a drive to apply innovative methods, tools, and technology to learning.

Why News Corp Australia

Join a culture where innovation is our operating rhythm. You’ll work on a diverse portfolio of Australia’s most loved brands and play a pivotal role in leading the technological shift in how we create commercial content. Our vibrant, hybrid work environment and dynamic, values-driven culture empowers you to push boundaries, collaborate as one unified team, and make a positive, measurable impact for all Australians.

News Extras Benefits is a commitment to our people for their valued contribution to News, our audiences, clients and communities.

Our benefits include:

Exclusively ours - Access to publications and products from across the News Corp family

Perks & discounts - Exclusive offers across a range of products and services that deliver genuine cost savings and benefits

Health & wellbeing - Advice, support and facilities for healthy minds and bodies (including an onsite gym at our Adelaide, Brisbane & Sydney offices)

Time off - News offers a range of leave options to support your personal needs and moments that matter in life: Parental leave, Birthday leave, Volunteer leave, Career break and Compassionate leave.

Financial wellbeing - Support on making the most of your money

In addition to our perks and benefits, employees have access to a number of initiatives and programs that support their wellbeing and professional growth, such as:

Helping you grow - Access tailored development opportunities, including leadership programs, to cultivate your skills and support your career

Giving back - Make a difference to our communities through volunteer opportunities and company-organised charity drives

Workplace Inclusion and facilities – Engage with your passions through various employee-resource groups and accessibility to multi-faith quiet rooms & parents’ rooms

Who are we?

This role is part of the Human Resources (HR) team where our people vision is to unlock News’ potential for growth in an ever-changing media landscape, we equip passionate people with the skills to excel, supported by an innovative and collaborative environment. Our people’s ideas and unique perspectives take centre stage, celebrated and delivered through the widest range of media brands and channels in Australia.

We’re focused on delivering the News Corp Australia strategy through delivering a future ready workforce and an innovative and dynamic workplace with automated, seamless and personalised experiences in a sustainable, high performing organisation

What’s next?

Apply now or for more information on careers at News Corp Australia visit www.newscorpaustralia.com/careers

Our Commitment to Diversity, Equity and Inclusion

Diversity strengthens our national teams and newsroom as we seek to tell stories that represent the communities of Australia. Our people make us unique, and so we are committed to equal employment opportunities regardless of race, religion, sexual orientation, age, disability or gender identity. Applicants of diverse backgrounds are encouraged to apply!

We are committed to ensuring an accessible and supportive recruitment process. If you require any adjustments or accommodations at any stage, please do not hesitate to let us know here. In order for us to assist you, please ensure you include the role title you are applying for in the subject line. For more information regarding Diversity at NewsCorp Australia please visit www.newscorpaustralia.com/careers under the Diversity@News section.

Job Title: Client Partner, Delivery Lead - Data & Technology
Ad Text:
We’ve been retained to appoint a Senior Client Partner to join a growing Sydney-based team, leading the delivery of complex programs while supporting key client relationships and growth.

This is a delivery-led role at its core. You will be responsible for overseeing and guiding programs of work end-to-end, ensuring quality outcomes, strong governance, and successful execution. Alongside delivery, you will contribute to account development and identify opportunities for expansion within existing clients.

The Role
You will play a pivotal role in leading delivery across multiple engagements, acting as the senior point of accountability for outcomes. While you will be aligned to key clients, the focus is on delivering high-quality work and building long-term trust through execution.

This is not a pure sales or account management role. Your credibility will come from your ability to lead delivery and consistently achieve results.

Key Responsibilities

Lead the delivery of complex programs across strategy and technology implementation
Take accountability for outcomes across the full delivery lifecycle
Provide oversight, structure, and governance across multiple engagements
Build strong, trusted relationships through consistent delivery excellence
Support account strategy and identify opportunities for growth within existing clients
Collaborate with sales and pursuit teams to shape opportunities where relevant

About You

You are a senior leader/manager with a strong track record of delivering programs, not just managing relationships.

Proven experience leading large-scale or complex program delivery
Background in consulting, ideally at Senior Manager or Principal level
Strong understanding of delivery frameworks, governance, and execution
Ability to engage stakeholders across both business and technology
Commercial awareness, with the ability to spot opportunities through delivery
Motivated by ownership, accountability, and delivering outcomes

Why This Role

This is an opportunity to step into a senior role where delivery is the foundation of your impact.

You’ll lead meaningful programs of work while contributing to the growth of key client relationships, without being purely sales-driven.

Apply:

Hello. We’re Virgin Australia. We’re the airline that’s always done things a little differently. Our way. The Virgin way. We’re on a mission to make flying better than ‘nice’. And to do that, it takes a team of talented people who are passionate about customer experience, doing the right thing always, with a focus on safety – in the air, on the ground and in our workplaces. A team who values real diversity and inclusion, in a big brand with big ambitions.

What you’ll be doing…

As a Senior Business Analyst, you will play a key role within our Commercial IT portfolio, supporting the delivery of technology initiatives that enable commercial strategy and drive business value.

This role will primarily focus on initiatives across ancillary products and services, helping to enhance the customer journey while supporting revenue growth. You will also contribute to a broader range of commercial and technology projects across the business.

You will act as the bridge between business and technology, leading end-to-end Business Analysis activities across complex initiatives, ensuring solutions are aligned to both customer and commercial outcomes.

This role is based in our Brisbane Head Office. 

You will….

Lead end-to-end stakeholder engagement and requirements gathering across small to large, complex projects
Analyze business problems, map current processes and define future-state solutions that improve efficiency and customer outcomes
Support delivery of commercial and ancillary initiatives, identifying opportunities to optimize revenue, conversion and customer experience
Develop high-quality documentation including business requirements, process maps and solution designs
Facilitate workshops, interviews and discovery sessions to align stakeholders and drive clarity
Own Business Analysis deliverables, ensuring outputs are completed on time and to a high standard
Collaborate closely with Project Managers, Solution Architects and delivery teams to support planning, solution design and testing
Maintain requirements traceability and ensure delivered solutions meet business needs
Translate complex technical concepts into clear, actionable insights for non-technical stakeholders
Build strong relationships across Technology and the broader business, acting as a trusted advisor
Identify opportunities to streamline processes, reduce duplication and support continuous improvement

You will be great in this role if you have…

5+ years’ experience as a Business Analyst, ideally across complex, fast-paced environments
Strong experience leading stakeholder engagement and end-to-end requirements gathering

About the role
Chariot Corporation Ltd (ASX: CC9) is a lithium exploration company with a growing portfolio of assets across Nigeria and the United States. As we scale rapidly toward our ambition of becoming a leading lithium supplier, we're looking for a Corporate Development Analyst to join our Perth-based team.

You'll work side-by-side with senior executives, directly influencing M&A activity, capital markets strategy, and high-value partnerships — all while building a career with tangible, visible impact in one of the world's most critical supply chains

This is an opportunity to work side-by-side with senior executives, directly influence M&A, capital markets and strategic partnerships—and build a career with real impact.

What you'll be doing

Build financial models and valuation analyses for investments, JV structures, and transactions

Support due diligence across legal, financial, and operational dimensions

Contribute to deal execution, including contract negotiation and project management

Draft ASX announcements, investor materials, and disclosure documents

Support the legal and company secretarial teams on compliance and governance

Stay sharp on market trends, regulatory shifts, and risks shaping the global lithium industry




What we're looking for

Bachelor's degree in Finance, Economics, Law, Engineering (other mining related technical field) with strong academic results

1–5 years' experience in corporate development, investment banking, strategy consulting, or resources

Strong financial modelling and Excel skills—comfortable running valuations and scenario analysis

Strong communication skills—clear, concise, and confident in stakeholder settings

Experience in mining, energy, or professional services is highly regarded

Australian citizenship or permanent residency required

What we offer


Competitive base salary + performance bonuses and equity incentives

Exposure to transformational M&A and growth projects

Hands-on experience across mining, capital markets, and strategy

Opportunities for international travel (USA, West Africa)

A lean, ambitious team where your impact is visible and valued


About us
Chariot Resources Limited is a mineral exploration company focused on discovering and developing high-grade and near surface lithium opportunities focused principally in the United States and Nigeria.




In addition to the recently announced acquisition of a Nigerian lithium portfolio, Chariot has twelve (12) lithium projects in the United States.


Chariot’s USA projects include the Black Mountain Project (which is prospective for hard rock lithium) in Wyoming, USA and the Resurgent Project (which is prospective for claystone lithium) in Nevada and Oregon, USA.


The Nigerian portfolio of hard-rock lithium assets consists of four project clusters (Fonlo, Gbugbu, Iganna, and Saki) in the Oyo and Kwara states which cover approximately 254 square kilometers and are comprised of 8 exploration licences and 2 small-scale mining leases. These assets represent one of the largest portfolios of lithium assets in the country and have a history of significant artisanal lithium mining.

Chariot holds a portfolio interest in certain properties prospective for claystone hosted lithium located in the State of Nevada in the United States through its interest in Mustang Lithium LLC.
